problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
hypothesis: The key to Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? is Dopamine/serotonin reuptake inhibition with specificity — specifically Dopamine/serotonin reuptake inhibition — which would help 12,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Longitudinal patient cohort with 20 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The important thing is not to stop questioning. — Einstein
